title: HJR 203 was filed
summary: The House property-tax proposal that became CS/CS/HJR 203 entered the 2026 regular-session bill record.
importance: This created the primary House vehicle tracked in the 2026 elimination debate.

title: CS/CS/HJR 203 passed the House
summary: The House passed CS/CS/HJR 203 by an 80-30 vote.
importance: The proposal cleared one chamber but still needed Senate approval to reach the ballot.

title: CS/CS/HJR 203 was referred to Senate Appropriations
summary: After House passage, the Senate received the proposal and referred it to Appropriations.
importance: Senate committee movement became the remaining regular-session path for that proposal.

title: CS/CS/HJR 203 died in Senate Appropriations
summary: The House-passed property-tax proposal did not advance through the Senate before the end of the regular session.
importance: This ended the regular-session path for CS/CS/HJR 203.

title: Senate memo described Special Session D legislation
summary: The Senate memo identified expected Special Session D legislation on redistricting, artificial intelligence, public records, and medical freedom.
importance: The official memo did not describe a property-tax elimination vehicle among the listed Special Session D items.

title: Special Session D bill-actions list showed no tracked property-tax elimination proposal
summary: The April 28 Senate bill-actions list reviewed for this tracker showed Special Session D activity on redistricting, AI, public records, medical freedom, and state reapportionment items.
importance: This supports the current status label that no tracked property-tax elimination proposal was active on that reviewed list.
